title: Understand: Congress: The Founders' Vision and Congress Today
description: Check your understanding of the Founders' vision of Congress and how Congress operates today with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: Congress—The Founders' Vision and Congress Today
Understand: Congress: The Founders' Vision and Congress Today

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which landmark Supreme Court decision focused on Congress’s authority to create a national bank?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Choose the names that best complete the sentence.**

\_\_\_\_ believed that Congress had the power to create a national bank, while \_\_\_\_ believed it did not.

[[☃ radio 1]]

**In a landmark Supreme Court decision, Chief Justice John Marshall concluded that, although there was no specific clause in the Constitution granting Congress the power to create a bank, \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the nation did not need a national bank
it was unconstitutional for Congress to create a bank
Congress could call for a national election to see whether the people were in favor of creating a bank
Congress could create one because doing so was consistent with other powers listed in the Constitution

assessment_items.question_content:
**In *McCulloch v.  Maryland,* Chief Justice Marshall applied the Supremacy Clause of Article VI of the Constitution, concluding  that \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: state law was superior to federal law.
Maryland did not have the power to tax the national bank because federal law was superior to state law
in cases related to economic policy, the Constitution was not always the supreme law of the land
Maryland could tax the national bank

assessment_items.question_content:
**During the 1930s, Congress’s power to regulate commerce was challenged in several Supreme Court cases concerning what?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**In *Wickard v. Filburn* (1942), the Supreme Court addressed Congress’s authority to do what?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Change the number of justices on the Supreme Court
Regulate the amount of wheat that farmers could grow
Change the prices of oil and gas

assessment_items.question_content:
**During which era did the Supreme Court interpret the Commerce Clause to grant Congress the broadest authority to regulate commerce?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**In what case did the Supreme Court strike down the Gun-Free School Zones Act on the grounds that Congress had exceeded its authority to regulate commerce?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**During which time period did the Supreme Court strike down the most federal laws?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**After a bill is introduced to the floor of the House of Representatives or the Senate, what is the next step in the traditional legislative process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Assigning the bill to a committee
Voting in the House or Senate
Sending the bill to the president

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the final step for a bill to become a law?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Assigning the bill to a committee
Sending the bill to the American people for a vote
Sending the bill to the president

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which choice best describes a filibuster?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The act of changing the boundaries of an electoral district to favor one party over another
A petition signed by members of the House of Representatives to bring a bill out of committee and onto the floor for a  vote
A tactic used by senators to block a bill by continuing to hold the floor and speak
The bringing of formal charges against a government officer for alleged crimes or abuses of power
